---
title: Relative rural wage gains outpace nonrural
description: Relative change in real average annual wages for rural and nonrural counties,
  indexed to a 2007 pre-recession baseline.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-x-wage-change-since-gr-lc/
categories: []
tags: []
image: X_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: Relative rural wage gains outpace nonrural
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Tracks real average annual wages (2023 dollars, BLS CPI-U-RS deflated) indexed to 2007 for rural and nonrural counties, showing that rural wage growth rates have exceeded nonrural on a relative basis.](./X_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

Tracks real average annual wages (2023 dollars, BLS CPI-U-RS deflated) indexed to 2007 for rural and nonrural counties, showing that rural wage growth rates have exceeded nonrural on a relative basis.

## Key Findings

- Rural real wages grew faster in relative terms than nonrural wages since 2007.
- The relative catch-up reflects a lower rural wage base — absolute gaps in dollar terms remain wide.
- Both rural and nonrural saw real wage growth accelerate after 2020 amid tight labor markets.
- 2025 values use Q2 quarterly QCEW data and may be revised in annual releases.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/wage_change_lc.R` in the producing project.
